steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The sentence opens: ‘A painting by 19th-century artist Katsushika Hokusai,’ — an introductory noun phrase followed by a comma. The main clause that follows must have that painting as its subject.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — deeper thematic meaning is conveyed to viewers of *The Great Wave off Kanagawa* through visual allegory.: breaks the grammatical link from the introductory phrase. ✗
  • Choice B — *The Great Wave off Kanagawa*'s deeper thematic meaning is conveyed to viewers through visual allegory.: same error as A. ✗
  • Choice C — visual allegory allows *The Great Wave off Kanagawa* to convey its deeper thematic meaning to viewers.: still doesn’t continue from the introduction of the painting. ✗
  • Choice D — *The Great Wave off Kanagawa* employs visual allegory to convey its deeper thematic meaning to viewers.: the painting itself is the subject of the main clause, correctly completing the introductory phrase. ✓
D is correct.
